nodes.title: Validate input is an array
nodes.why: This prevents invalid inputs from silently producing confusing results.
nodes.alternative: Return an empty array for non-array inputs.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter behavior, but easier to debug when input is wrong.

nodes.title: Clone before writing to keep it immutable
nodes.why: The original array stays untouched, which is important for state updates and predictable behavior.
nodes.tradeoff: Safer immutable updates, with the cost of allocating a new array.

nodes.title: Return unchanged copy for out-of-range indexes
nodes.why: Invalid indexes should not mutate data, so returning the copy keeps behavior stable and explicit.
nodes.alternative: Throw an error when index is out of range.
nodes.tradeoff: More forgiving API, but callers must check if an update actually happened.
